title: Dynamic Routing in RAG Systems
text: Dynamic routing is a technique for intelligently selecting between different retrieval strategies based on query characteristics. In RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems, dynamic routing can significantly improve answer quality and latency by choosing the optimal combination of vector search and knowledge graph traversal.

title: Knowledge Graphs for Information Retrieval
text: Knowledge graphs enable structured representation of entities and their relationships. They are particularly useful for explainability and handling complex multi-hop queries that require understanding of entity connections and semantic relationships.

title: Vector Databases and Semantic Search
text: Vector databases store embeddings of documents and enable fast semantic similarity search. They excel at handling natural language queries and finding contextually relevant documents even when exact keyword matches are not available.

title: Multi-User Systems and Session Management
text: Managing user sessions in multi-user systems requires careful handling of authentication tokens, session state, and audit logging. Security and user isolation are critical concerns in production deployments.
